openmeter/credit/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapters for the credit domain: balanceSnapshotRepo persists balance snapshots and grantDBADapter persists grants, implementing balance.SnapshotRepo and grant.Repo. Primary constraint: every write path must be transaction-aware via entutils.TransactingRepo.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping on every write
patterns.description: All mutating methods wrap their body with entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound Ent transaction is honoured.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue(ctx, b, func(ctx context.Context, rep *balanceSnapshotRepo) error { return rep.db.BalanceSnapshot.Update()...Exec(ctx) })

patterns.name: TxCreator + TxUser[T] confined to transaction.go
patterns.description: Each repo struct implements Tx(ctx), WithTx(ctx, *entutils.TxDriver), Self() in the single transaction.go file using db.HijackTx + db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig.
patterns.example: func (e *grantDBADapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) grant.Repo { txClient := db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return NewPostgresGrantRepo(txClient.Client()) }

patterns.name: Soft-delete via clock.Now()
patterns.description: Deletes set DeletedAt/VoidedAt via clock.Now()/at, never hard delete. Queries filter DeletedAtIsNil() OR DeletedAtGT(now) explicitly.

patterns.name: db.IsNotFound mapped to domain errors
patterns.description: Convert Ent not-found to the domain error (credit.GrantNotFoundError, balance.NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError). Never surface raw Ent errors.

patterns.name: mapXxxEntity canonical conversion + UTC normalization
patterns.description: Each file defines mapGrantEntity / mapBalanceSnapshotEntity that translate *db.X to the domain struct, normalizing times to time.UTC.

key_file_guides.role: Implements balance.SnapshotRepo: InvalidateAfter (soft-deletes snapshots after a time), GetLatestValidAt, Save (CreateBulk).
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetLatestValidAt orders ByAt(desc) then ByUpdatedAt(desc) to deterministically pick newest among same-timestamp duplicates; keep all three methods TransactingRepo-wrapped.

key_file_guides.role: Implements grant.Repo: CreateGrant, VoidGrant (soft via SetVoidedAt), ListGrants (limit/offset or Page), ListActiveGrantsBetween, GetGrant, DeleteOwnerGrants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateGrant/VoidGrant have TODO about transaction+locking. ListActiveGrantsBetween silently ignores negative-amount grants (AmountGTE(0.0)). Soft-delete filters are duplicated across predicates — keep them consistent.

key_file_guides.role: Single file implementing TxCreator + TxUser[T] for both grantDBADapter and balanceSnapshotRepo via HijackTx (ReadOnly:false) and NewTxClientFromRawConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HijackTx must always use ReadOnly:false. Do not duplicate Tx/WithTx/Self in other files.

anti_patterns: Calling the raw db.Client inside a helper without TransactingRepo wrapping — bypasses ctx-bound transactions.
Hard-deleting grant or balance snapshot rows — the domain is soft-delete only.
Surfacing raw Ent not-found/constraint errors to callers instead of domain error types.
Implementing Tx/WithTx/Self outside transaction.go.
Accepting *entdb.Tx as a struct field or parameter instead of using entutils.TransactingRepo.

decisions.decision: balanceSnapshotRepo and grantDBADapter each implement the full TxCreator+TxUser contract separately rather than sharing a base struct.
decisions.rationale: The two repos have different TxUser[T] type parameters, so a shared base struct is not type-safe in Go generics; keeping them side by side in transaction.go keeps the pattern visible.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a transaction-aware write method to balanceSnapshotRepo

openmeter/credit/balance.purpose: Domain types and service layer for credit balance snapshots: the in-memory Map (per-grant float64 balances), Snapshot struct, SnapshotRepo interface, and SnapshotService that transparently back-fills missing usage from streaming when a stored snapshot has zero usage.

patterns.name: SnapshotService wraps SnapshotRepo + usage back-fill
patterns.description: SnapshotService.GetLatestValidAt delegates to Repo then, if Snapshot.Usage.IsZero(), queries streaming via UsageQuerier to compute period usage. Use the service, not the raw Repo, when usage completeness matters.
patterns.example: snapshot, err := svc.GetLatestValidAt(ctx, owner, at) // fills usage if zero

patterns.name: service() sentinel guard
patterns.description: SnapshotService declares an unexported service() method so the raw SnapshotRepo cannot accidentally satisfy SnapshotService.

patterns.name: UNIQUE_COUNT needs a double streaming query
patterns.description: For MeterAggregationUniqueCount, QueryUsage queries (periodStart→to) and (periodStart→from) and subtracts via alpacadecimal; all other aggregations use a single from→to query.

patterns.name: NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError for missing snapshots
patterns.description: GetLatestValidAt returns NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError (not generic not-found) when no snapshot precedes the requested time; callers type-assert it.

patterns.name: Clone balance.Map before mutating
patterns.description: Map is a plain map[string]float64 (reference type). Clone() before mutating to avoid aliasing shared state.

key_file_guides.role: Value types: Map (grantID→float64) with Burn/Set/Clone/Balance/ExactlyForGrants, Snapshot (Map + Overage + At + SnapshottedUsage), NewStartingMap.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Map is a reference type — Clone before mutating. SnapshottedUsage.IsZero() drives the service back-fill path.

key_file_guides.role: SnapshotRepo interface + NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError domain error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetLatestValidAt comment notes the returned Snapshot may lack usage; the service layer fills it — don't call repo directly for complete usage.

key_file_guides.role: SnapshotService interface + concrete service; SnapshotServiceConfig wires OwnerConnector, StreamingConnector, Repo; UsageQuerier built inside NewSnapshotService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not pass UsageQuerier externally — it is constructed internally from config to bind the right closures.

key_file_guides.role: UsageQuerier interface + impl dispatching to streaming.Connector.QueryMeter with aggregation-specific logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: alpacadecimal used for UNIQUE_COUNT subtraction; getValueFromRows errors if >1 row. Don't switch to plain float64 for the unique-count case.

anti_patterns: Calling SnapshotRepo.GetLatestValidAt directly when complete usage is needed — go through SnapshotService.
Mutating a balance.Map without cloning first — aliasing corrupts shared engine state.
Using float64 arithmetic for UNIQUE_COUNT subtraction instead of alpacadecimal.
Returning a generic error instead of NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError when no snapshot exists.
Adding a new aggregation case without checking whether it needs a double query like UNIQUE_COUNT.

decisions.decision: SnapshotService transparently back-fills usage for snapshots persisted without usage data.
decisions.rationale: Earlier snapshots may lack usage fields; querying streaming at read time avoids a data migration.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing a SnapshotService with all required dependencies

openmeter/credit/driver.purpose: v1 HTTP handlers (creditdriver) for grant operations (ListGrants, VoidGrant, ListGrantsV2) on the v1 Chi router; a pure translation layer adapting HTTP request/response to credit.GrantConnector + grant.Repo with no business logic.

patterns.name: httptransport.HandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Each handler is a type alias of httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params] returned from a method on grantHandler, separating URL/query params from body decode.

patterns.name: Namespace via namespaceDecoder, never from request
patterns.description: Every handler resolves namespace through the injected NamespaceDecoder; failure returns an error. Namespace is never read from path/query/body.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ListGrantsHandlerRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: Dual pagination union for v1 backward compatibility
patterns.description: ListGrants returns commonhttp.Union[[]api.EntitlementGrant, pagination.Result[...]]: Option1 (plain array) when Page.IsZero(), else Option2; ListGrantsV2 always returns a paginated result.

patterns.name: Per-handler error encoder appended to shared options
patterns.description: Domain errors map to HTTP statuses inside each handler's error encoder via httptransport.AppendOptions over shared h.options.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(func(...) bool { return commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches[*pagination.InvalidError](ctx, http.StatusBadRequest, err, w) }))

key_file_guides.role: GrantHandler interface + grantHandler impl: ListGrants, VoidGrant, ListGrantsV2. Uses grantRepo for reads and grantConnector for VoidGrant mutation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListGrantsV2 resolves customer IDOrKey→ID via customerService.GetCustomer (cross-domain) and silently skips deleted customers. v2 always returns api.GrantV2PaginatedResponse with api.EntitlementGrantV2 (no dual format).

anti_patterns: Adding business logic here — the driver only translates HTTP to domain types.
Reading namespace from HTTP query/path/body instead of namespaceDecoder.
Returning raw Ent errors from the error encoder instead of commonhttp.NewHTTPError.
Using the v1 api.EntitlementGrant type in a v2 endpoint (v2 uses api.EntitlementGrantV2).
Adding a new endpoint here without first adding it to the TypeSpec source in api/spec/.

decisions.decision: ListGrants returns a union (array OR paginated) while ListGrantsV2 is always paginated.
decisions.rationale: The v1 API predates pagination and existing clients expect a plain array without page params; v2 was designed paginated from the start.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 grant handler following the HandlerWithArgs pattern

openmeter/credit/engine.purpose: Pure-logic grant burn-down engine: from a starting snapshot, grants, a QueryUsageFn callback, and optional reset times it produces an ending Snapshot plus a GrantBurnDownHistory. No Ent or HTTP dependencies — all I/O is the QueryUsageFn callback.

patterns.name: Engine.Run iterates periods between resets
patterns.description: Run builds a timeline from StartingSnapshot.At + reset times + Until, runs runBetweenResets per closed period, applies reset() between periods, and merges history segments into one GrantBurnDownHistory.
patterns.example: for idx, period := range timeline.GetClosedPeriods() { runRes, _ := e.runBetweenResets(...); if idx != last { snap, _ = e.reset(...) } }

patterns.name: burnPhase subdivision within a reset-free period
patterns.description: runBetweenResets subdivides each period into burnPhases via getPhases(): a new phase starts on grant activity change (effectiveAt/expiresAt/deletedAt/voidedAt) or grant recurrence; each phase burns down independently.
patterns.example: phases, _ := e.getPhases(grants, period, boundary); for _, phase := range phases { e.burnDownGrants(...) }

patterns.name: PrioritizeGrants three-pass stable sort
patterns.description: PrioritizeGrants applies three stable sorts in reverse importance: createdAt+id, then expiration (earlier first), then priority (lower first). Must run before burnDownGrants and re-run on priorityChange.
patterns.example: PrioritizeGrants(grants) // lower priority number burned first; earlier expiry burned first

patterns.name: alpacadecimal for burn arithmetic
patterns.description: burnDownGrants keeps intermediate balances as alpacadecimal.Decimal; only the final value calls InexactFloat64() to avoid float64 rounding.

patterns.name: RunParams.Clone() before storing in RunResult
patterns.description: Run clones params at the top so RunResult.RunParams is immutable and independent of the caller's slices.

patterns.name: Resets must be strictly after StartingSnapshot.At
patterns.description: Run errors if any reset time equals the snapshot time; the reset timeline must be (snapshot.At, Until].

key_file_guides.role: Engine interface, RunParams/RunResult, QueryUsageFn type, NewEngine. QueryUsageFn is the only external I/O.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeterAggregationLatest treats grant amounts as limits (balances reset to grant.Amount at every phase start) — distinct from budget behaviour.

key_file_guides.role: Engine.Run, runBetweenResets, and innermost burnDownGrants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: runBetweenResets validates grants pair exactly with balances (ExactlyForGrants) and errors otherwise; the grants slice is copied before sorting.

key_file_guides.role: getPhases merges activity-change and recurrence times into a sorted, deduplicated []burnPhase.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Times are Truncate(time.Minute); preserve this when adding new change-time sources.

key_file_guides.role: Helpers: getGrantActivityChanges, getGrantRecurrenceTimes, filterRelevantGrants, PrioritizeGrants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PrioritizeGrants is exported (used by reset.go); its three-pass stable sort is load-bearing — do not collapse into one comparator.

key_file_guides.role: GrantBurnDownHistory / GrantBurnDownHistorySegment value types; GetSnapshotAtStartOfSegment reconstructs a Snapshot at a boundary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Overage() assumes at least one segment exists — never call on empty history.

key_file_guides.role: engine.reset() applies RolloverBalance per grant and optionally burns carry-over overage.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Grants inactive at reset time are skipped during rollover — their balance is effectively zeroed for the next period.

anti_patterns: Introducing Ent or HTTP imports — the engine must remain a pure calculation library.
Using plain float64 arithmetic for grant burn subtraction instead of alpacadecimal.
Passing a balance.Map that does not exactly match the grants slice to runBetweenResets.
Replacing PrioritizeGrants' three-pass stable sort with a single-pass comparator.
Omitting Clone() on the grants slice before passing to the engine (it sorts in place).

decisions.decision: The engine is stateless and takes a QueryUsageFn callback rather than a streaming.Connector.
decisions.rationale: Decoupling from streaming makes the engine independently testable with a mock function and lets callers cache/pre-aggregate.

decisions.decision: Burn phases are computed per-period (between resets) rather than globally.
decisions.rationale: Resets change effective balances, so priority order and recurrence schedules must be re-evaluated after each reset; a global computation would conflate periods.

code_examples.scenario: Running the engine for a period

openmeter/credit/grant.purpose: Core domain types for the credit grant subsystem: the Grant value type, OwnerConnector and Repo interfaces, expiration/rollover logic, and Watermill grant lifecycle events. This is the contract layer adapter/engine/service depend on — no Ent or HTTP imports.

patterns.name: ActiveAt / GetEffectivePeriod as canonical activity window
patterns.description: GetEffectivePeriod() returns a StartBoundedPeriod bounded by the earliest of ExpiresAt, DeletedAt, VoidedAt; ActiveAt(t) delegates to it. Use these instead of comparing fields directly.

patterns.name: Repo embeds TxCreator + TxUser[Repo]
patterns.description: grant.Repo requires entutils.TxCreator and entutils.TxUser[Repo] so callers propagate transactions; implementations must satisfy Tx/WithTx/Self.
patterns.example: type Repo interface { CreateGrant(...) ...; entutils.TxCreator; entutils.TxUser[Repo] }

patterns.name: Versioned event literals (v2) over embedded domain
patterns.description: v2 events use a pinned primitive-only struct (grantEventV2GrantLiteral) with ToDomainGrant() conversion. New versions add a new named struct; never modify existing ones.
patterns.example: type CreatedEventV2 grantEventV2; func (e CreatedEventV2) EventName() string { return grantCreatedEventNameV2 }

patterns.name: ExpiresAt pre-computed at creation
patterns.description: Grant.ExpiresAt is set from GetExpiration() at creation (RepoCreateInput.ExpiresAt); query predicates use stored ExpiresAt rather than recomputing.

patterns.name: RolloverBalance vs RecurrenceBalance — distinct semantics
patterns.description: RolloverBalance clamps to [ResetMinRollover, ResetMaxRollover]; RecurrenceBalance always resets to Amount. Do not conflate.

key_file_guides.role: Grant struct, GetEffectivePeriod, ActiveAt, RolloverBalance, RecurrenceBalance, GetExpiration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RolloverBalance clamps to [ResetMinRollover, ResetMaxRollover]; RecurrenceBalance always resets to Amount. GetEffectivePeriod upper bound = first non-nil of ExpiresAt/DeletedAt/VoidedAt.

key_file_guides.role: OwnerConnector interface (DescribeOwner, GetResetTimelineInclusive, GetUsagePeriodStartAt, ...), Owner value type, ResetBehavior, OwnerNotFoundError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetResetTimelineInclusive returns the start of the containing period for each time point — the first returned time may precede period.From.

key_file_guides.role: Repo interface, ListParams, RepoCreateInput, OrderBy constants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Limit/Offset on ListParams are deprecated — prefer Page-based pagination. OrderByDefault is OrderByCreatedAt.

key_file_guides.role: ExpirationPeriod struct + GetExpiration(t) for day/month/year durations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Unknown duration values return zero time.Time silently — validate ExpirationPeriodDuration before persisting.

key_file_guides.role: v1 Watermill events (CreatedEvent, VoidedEvent) — deprecated, embed Grant directly. EventSubsystem = "credit".
key_file_guides.watch_for: v1 embeds the domain Grant, making serialization fragile to domain changes; use v2 literal pattern for new events.

key_file_guides.role: v2 events using grantEventV2GrantLiteral (primitive fields) + ToDomainGrant().
key_file_guides.watch_for: When adding a grant field needed in events, add to grantEventV2GrantLiteral AND ToDomainGrant(), and create a new versioned struct rather than editing existing ones.

anti_patterns: Comparing grant.ExpiresAt/DeletedAt directly instead of using ActiveAt/GetEffectivePeriod.
Creating a new event version by modifying the existing grantEventV2 struct.
Implementing Repo without embedding TxCreator and TxUser[Repo].
Using Limit/Offset in new list code instead of pagination.Page.
Using unknown ExpirationPeriodDuration values — GetExpiration returns zero time silently.

decisions.decision: ExpiresAt is pre-computed and stored at creation rather than derived dynamically.
decisions.rationale: The engine queries grants by ExpiresAt; computing it in SQL would duplicate expiration arithmetic, so pre-storing keeps queries simple and indexable.

decisions.decision: v2 events use a primitive-only literal struct instead of embedding the Grant domain struct.
decisions.rationale: Embedding Grant (v1) means domain changes break event serialization; the v2 literal is decoupled and independently versionable.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v2 grant event type
